questions.question: Explain the binary number system with its advantages. Why is it used in digital systems?
keyPoints: Base-2 number system using digits 0 and 1
Advantages: noise immunity, simple circuits, reliable storage
questions.modelAnswer: Binary number system uses base-2 with only two digits: 0 and 1. Each bit position has weight 2^n where n is position from right starting at 0. Example: (1011)_2 = 8+2+1 = 11. Advantages: (1) Two states are easily distinguishable - HIGH/LOW voltage, (2) Simple electronic implementation using switches/transistors, (3) Noise immune - large margin between states, (4) Direct mapping to Boolean algebra (AND, OR, NOT). Used in all digital systems from simple calculators to supercomputers.

questions.question: Convert the following to binary: (a) (45)_10 (b) (127)_10 (c) (200)_10
keyPoints: Collect remainders from bottom to top
Verify by converting back to decimal

questions.question: Define the following terms with examples: (a) Bit (b) Nibble (c) Byte (d) MSB (e) LSB
What is the range of values that can be represented by an n-bit unsigned binary number? Calculate for n = 4, 8, and 16.
Convert (101.11)_2 to decimal. Explain binary fractions.
keyPoints: Integer part: use positive powers of 2
Fractional part: use negative powers of 2
